resources.string: Remove “%1$s” from the open directory?\n\nThe real directory will not be deleted.
Please configure a model first in Settings → Model management
Standard thinking, balanced speed and quality
Maximum thinking, for the hardest problems
Please configure a model in settings first
Start the session with a question
Thinking depth, tone, prompts and reasoning preservation
Enter key sends or inserts a newline
Provider, key, model ID and default model
Workspace tool toggles, SSH execution and local execution target
Image understanding model and search API
Agent, MCP, Skills and LineCode extensions
Theme mode, custom colors and high-contrast appearance
Code wrap, page opening method and built-in browser JS
When on, any HTTP address is allowed instead of being locked to HTTPS
Chats, config, diff and workspace usage
View and add long-term, project and short-term memory
Full export, .linecode import and data archive
Wake Lock, foreground service and battery whitelist
Version, diagnostics and open source licenses
Experimental features such as accessibility phone control
Control this device via accessibility service
Accessibility service is required to control the phone

The phone control feature is implemented through the Android Accessibility Service and can perform automated operations such as tapping, swiping, screenshots, and viewing UI hierarchy. Please read the following carefully before enabling: This feature is experimental and is intended only to assist developers with automated testing and debugging on this device; all actions are actively authorized and decided by you, and the app will not control the system without your consent; automated control may cause accidental touches, data modification, or other unexpected behavior, and you assume the related risks; please save important data before operating and avoid using it in scenarios involving payments, privacy, or critical systems; this app is not liable for any direct or indirect losses caused by using this feature; this feature is provided without any warranty, and the developer reserves the right to adjust, restrict, or disable it at any time. If you do not agree with the above, please do not enable or use this feature.
Click View by id, text or coordinates
Allow Back, Home, Recents, notifications and exit app
Allow reading the current window hierarchy
Phone control requires an image understanding model. Please configure it in tool settings.
Rigorous and professional, code-first, no emoji
Friendly, like chatting with a friend, emoji allowed
Edit name, key and model ID
Select multiple models to batch delete
You will need to re-add it to use it again
Manually pick protocol, Base URL, model ID and key
OpenAI compatible protocol must end with /v1, e.g. https://api.example.com/v1; do not enter just the domain and do not add /chat/completions.
DeepSeek uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /v1.
GLM uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /api/paas/v4.
Mimo uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /v1.
Mimo Token Plan uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /v1.
Kimi/Moonshot uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /v1.
Qwen/DashScope uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /compatible-mode/v1.
OpenAI Chat Completions compatible models use /v1, do not add /chat/completions.
Claude uses Anthropic Messages API, Base URL is the root address, do not add /v1/messages.
Gemini OpenAI compatible endpoint ends with /v1beta/openai, the app will call /models and /chat/completions.
OpenRouter uses OpenAI compatible protocol, Base URL ends with /api/v1.
Codex uses Responses API, Base URL ends with /v1, do not add /responses.
Pick via SAF, the app will import a local copy
Auto mode prefers Android Hexagon NPU, falls back to CPU if unsupported
Test successful. Response time: %1$d ms. Model returned data.
Test successful. Response time: %1$d ms. Model returned empty content.
Please fill in Base URL, API Key and Model ID before testing

### Markdown preview
- Lists, bold and links render with message styling
- Code wrap toggle immediately affects the code block below

| Type | Status |
| --- | --- |
| Table | Enabled |
| Link | [LineCode](https://line.local) |

```java
String path = "/storage/emulated/0/.linecode/home/very/long/workspace/path/that/needs/wrap/Main.java";
System.out.println("code wrap preview: " + path);
```
Allow tools and agents to access any path, not limited to the workspace
Enabling this option allows AI to access any file on the device, including system files and app data. Only enable this if you trust the current AI model and agents. Continue?
HTTP restrictions and built-in browser script execution
Light thinking, suited for simple tasks
Standard thinking, balanced speed and quality
Deep thinking, suited for complex tasks
Maximum thinking, for the hardest problems
Enable auto skills, long-term memory, project memory, short-term memory and chat indexing
Edit system, tone, session, learning memory and context compaction templates
Send historical reasoning back to compatible models, good for multi-round tool calls
No models yet. Tap + to add OpenAI compatible, Codex or Anthropic models.
No models yet. Please add OpenAI compatible, Codex or Anthropic models in model management first.
Please enter the local model form from “Load local model”.
Please go back and open the custom model form.
Please query and select a model first
Local file picker will be added later.
Recommended to start from 4096 on phones; model ID will auto-tag context after saving.
Supports k / m units (case-insensitive). Leave empty to fall back to the model default.
When enabled, prefers the provider\'s compaction model; OpenAI/Codex both go through the OpenAI Responses path, not Chat Completions.
Failed to fetch model list. Please check Base URL and API Key.
Please select a GGUF file first. Local inference will be added later.
Please enter name and model ID
Tool call limit must be -1 or a non-negative integer
Please select or enter a compaction model ID
Codex uses the Responses API and must end with /v1, e.g. https://api.example.com/v1; do not add /responses.
Anthropic protocol must end with /anthropic, e.g. https://api.example.com/anthropic; do not add /v1/messages.
OpenAI compatible protocol must end with /v1, e.g. https://api.example.com/v1; do not enter just the domain and do not add /chat/completions.
Open the legacy add page, manually pick protocol and fill Base URL, model ID and key
Import a GGUF file via the system file picker and enable NPU on supported Android devices
Export database, chats, settings and .linecode workspace as ZIP
Restore from ZIP, overwriting database, chats, configs and workspace files
Control whether Enter sends the message or inserts a newline
When off, code can be scrolled horizontally
Open pages in the system browser
When off, in-app pages will not run scripts

The image_understanding tool reads local or SSH workspace images and returns the image content based on the prompt using the model selected here. Supports OpenAI compatible, Codex Responses and Anthropic Messages protocols.
The image_generation tool calls the image model selected here to generate images. Generated images are returned as inline Markdown images and shown directly in the chat.
Configure the web search backend used by the web_search tool
e.g. basic, advanced, google; can be empty

Local workspace runs file read/write, file search, Agent, web search, image understanding and image generation inside the current workspace path. Search API and image models are configured in Tool settings.

SSH Shell mode disables local file read/write and file search. Agent, Agent pipeline, todos, web search, image understanding and image generation remain available. File operations should be performed via shell commands inside the SSH environment.
SSH Shell can connect to a remote Linux server, desktop dev machine, NAS, or Termux on the phone itself. Termux is a separate option and is not the only target.
Querying will request tools/list and show the MCP tool list.
Fill in the URL and query the tools exposed by the service.
After querying, the tools list will appear here. You can enable or disable each one.
Querying the tools exposed by the service.
MCP URL must start with http:// or https://
Please enter a name and a valid MCP URL
Please click "Query MCP list" to fetch tools before saving
Used to trigger and identify the custom Agent. Will be normalized to a lowercase English identifier on save.
Describe this Agent\'s role, boundaries, output format and acceptance criteria
e.g. Triggered when the user asks to fix tests, analyze performance, or refactor components
After describing the goal, the form will be filled automatically.
Please describe the Agent requirements first
Please enter name, identifier and prompt
e.g. I want an Agent that analyzes Android native View performance issues, reads the related View and Presenter, locates bottlenecks, then suggests fixes.
Save and export together with the current project
Save to LineCode\'s app-private global directory
Android native view spec for the current project
Tap the install and create entry
Create a SKILL.md, or install from a local directory, SKILL.md, or ZIP to the project or global Skills folder.
LineCode native extensions will be added later.
Custom Agents can be toggled on or off. When disabled, they are excluded from the prompt and tool calls.
Custom MCPs can query tools/list. After saving, they are exposed to the AI as extension tools.
Open Termux and paste to run the grant command, then come back to grant RUN_COMMAND.
Current Context is not an Activity. Please open this page from the app\'s main UI.
If the system did not show a dialog, please allow "Run commands in Termux environment" in app permissions.
Make sure the grant command has been executed and Termux remains runnable.
Installing openssh via Termux RUN_COMMAND, generating key and starting sshd. The first run may take a long time.
Markdown block and inline grammar parser. The POM declares the license as BSD-2-Clause.
GitHub Flavored Markdown table extension, inheriting the BSD-2-Clause license from commonmark-java.
SSH2 Java implementation. The POM declares JSch and JZlib as Revised BSD; jBCrypt portions are ISC.
In-app vector drawable icons are converted from Lucide SVGs in LineAI node_modules. Lucide is ISC; icons derived from Feather are MIT.
Connect a remote Linux server and use it as the execution target
Add a custom HTTP header to every MCP request
Fetch the MCP tool list and pick which ones to enable
Let the current model draft the name, identifier and prompt
Describe what the Agent should do
Describe the agent\'s role and behavior
Define reusable AI agents with custom prompts and tools
Connect external MCP servers and pick tools
Install SKILL.md files to teach the model new behaviors
Add a new item to this extension type
No Skills discovered. You can create or install a SKILL.md.
LineCode extensions will be added later.
Pick a file and then choose project or global install location
Create a new SKILL.md in the project or global skills directory
Manually enter the path of a directory, SKILL.md or .zip
Edit config, prompt, headers or tool list
You will need to re-add or re-install it to use it again
Allows the app to act as a terminal provider for LineCode
Connect external terminal provider apps for shell execution and file transfer
Scan for available terminal provider apps
No terminal providers found. Install a provider app and try again.
This app will be used for shell execution and file transfer
Execute shell commands and transfer files via external terminal provider app
Connect a remote server via SSH and use it as the workspace
Run shell commands through Termux and integrate with its package manager
Install Termux and run pkg install openssh once
Set a password with passwd so the app can ssh into it
Run sshd on a port (default 8022) and enable Termux:Wake Lock
Use {{variable}} for placeholders. Saved templates apply on the next model request. Reset restores the built-in template.
No TODO items yet. Use the todo_update tool to maintain the list.
Please complete the basic setup for project, model, permissions and tools before starting.
For first-time users, walk through the basic configuration step by step
For experienced developers, covering architecture, protocols, extensions and security details
Tutorial content failed to load. Please reinstall LineCode Pro and try again.
Keep CPU and screen awake while generating or compacting
When on, a coding-in-progress notification stays visible
Start a silent AudioTrack during background tasks
Allow notifications so the keep-alive service can stay visible.
Open the Android whitelist request screen
Track app data usage in real time
Project files, skills and extension data
Patches and comparison results generated by tool calls
Models, theme, MCP and system settings
The saved theme is applied as the custom theme.
Close to GitHub\'s dark code UI
Edit and save your own color theme
LineCode coding task background keep-alive notification

Welcome to LineCode Pro. This application is an on-device AI coding workspace that provides code conversation, file operations, tool calls, and optional SSH/Termux integration through large language models. By using this service, you indicate that you have read and understood the entire content of this agreement and voluntarily assume the responsibilities associated with using this service. You should independently review any code, commands, or suggestions generated by this application, and must not use automated outputs directly in production environments or critical systems, nor use this application for illegal, infringing, or network security-harming activities. This application may access local project directories you select, execute shell commands, or connect to remote servers you configure; related operations are actively authorized and controlled by you. Please keep sensitive information such as API keys, SSH private keys, and passwords safe. We will not collect, upload, or share your code, chat records, or personally identifiable information without your permission; however, network requests you actively initiate (such as model API calls, web searches, MCP services) will be processed according to the terms of the corresponding third-party services. Content generated by the application is for reference only and does not constitute professional legal, medical, financial, or technical advice. This application is provided "as is," and the developer is not liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or punitive damages arising from the use or inability to use this service. The developer reserves the right to modify this agreement at any time, and the revised agreement will be published within the application; if you continue to use this application, it is deemed that you accept the revised agreement. If you do not agree with this agreement, please stop using and uninstall this application immediately.
Please agree to the user agreement before continuing to use LineCode Pro.
Content is large, only part may be copied
Open Settings → Model management → Add model, then send a message.
LineCode native Java View version. This page mirrors LineAI\'s Android UI structure.
Choose how to add a model. The form and local model picker will be wired up next.
Manage the install, enabled state and deletion of Agent, MCP and Skill extensions.
This page already plugs into the native navigation and shared style. Real settings items and business logic will be added later.
Business logic to be wired up
No model available, unable to run the agent pipeline.
Agent pipeline has a circular dependency or duplicate id, unable to execute.
View complete error stacks, HTTP requests, and responses
Missing \"manage all files\" permission, unable to access the external workspace. Please allow LineCode to manage all files in system settings and retry.
Missing file read/write permission, unable to access the external workspace. Please allow LineCode to read and write storage and retry.
AI generation failed: no result returned
Found %1$d models, please import them in model management
Read, write, edit and delete files
Control this device via the accessibility service
Maintain the current session TODO list; the state is injected into the system prompt
Read local or SSH workspace images and call the selected vision model to understand the content
Call the selected image model to generate images, returned as inline Markdown images
Search the internet and view web pages
Run shell commands via the terminal provider IPC
Export the current chat as Markdown or share it
Summarize earlier context as a hidden summary
Automatically run enabled tools; dangerous tools are confirmed per policy
Dangerous operations require confirmation before running
Only read, search and list directory are allowed; writing and shell are disabled
Summarize earlier context as a hidden summary; old messages remain in history.
No model available. Please configure a model before compacting context.
Reading terminal provider directory via IPC…
Use ~/.linecode/skills in the SSH shell to manage SSH Skills.
AI returned config missing name, slug, or prompt.
AI did not return valid JSON.
These paths are from files selected via the button on the left of the input field; do not repeat them in your response unless the user explicitly asks.

You are an exploration Agent in a remote Shell environment. You can use shell_execute to execute read-only commands.
Rules:
- Only read code, do not make any modifications.
- Prefer read-only tools to search and read key files; you can use shell_execute to execute read-only commands.

You are a coding Agent in a remote Shell environment (the current workspace is on an SSH remote or terminal provider container; local file_read / file_write / file_edit / glob / list_dir may not be available).
Rules:
- Most work must be done via shell_execute: read with cat/ls/grep, write with sed/awk/python heredoc or tee, verify with cat.
- Only modify files or directories listed in write_scope; when there is no write_scope, writing to files is prohibited.

You are a code exploration Agent. Your task is to quickly locate and analyze code.
Rules:
- Only read code, do not make any modifications, and do not call any write tools.
- Prefer read-only tools to search and read key files; you can use shell_execute to execute read-only commands.
- Provide concise and accurate answers.

You are a coding Agent. Your task is to complete well-scoped coding subtasks.
Rules:
- Only modify files or directories listed in write_scope; when there is no write_scope, writing to files is prohibited.
- Prefer file_read / file_write / file_edit / glob / list_dir to complete work; only use shell_execute when file tools truly cannot meet the need.
